svg.text: OptiCrew — Client Booking to Feedback Flow
Booking · admin review · team allocation · rejection cascade · execution · dual feedback

g.text: b. Iterate available pool of OptimizationTeams
members see task in their schedule
Required: reason from fixed list · budget remaining (3 / month)
📨 NOTIFY ADMIN (FYI · low): "Emp X rejected Task Y (reason). Cascade in progress."
Find another employee whose task can be exchanged with the rejected one.
Both feasibilities (skill, no time conflict) must pass.
Workload preserved both sides · makespan preserved · no compensation.
📨 NOTIFY ADMIN (FYI · low): "Auto-resolved: swap between Emp A ↔ Emp B."
📨 NOTIFY both swapped employees: "Your task changed."
→ if no valid pair found, fall through to Try 2a
Find an employee with a mid-day gap at the rejected task's hour.
Receiver fills the gap · day-end doesn't move · no compensation.
📨 NOTIFY ADMIN (FYI · low): "Auto-resolved: Task Y placed in Emp B's gap."
📨 NOTIFY receiving employee: "Task added to your day."
→ if only end-of-day "gaps" exist, fall through to Try 2b
Rank candidates by computed estimated completion time (earliest first).
Admin sends offer · employee opts in · compensation applies.
Earliest-finish ranking minimizes the makespan extension.
📨 NOTIFY ADMIN (medium): "Stretch offer required for Task Y. N candidates ranked."
📨 NOTIFY chosen candidate: "Optional task offered + compensation. Accept / Decline?"
📨 NOTIFY ADMIN on decline (medium): "Emp B declined. Try next?"
→ if all candidates decline, fall through to Try 3
Admin handles directly: override-assign, reschedule, or cancel.
📨 NOTIFY ADMIN (high · email): "Auto-cascade exhausted on Task Y. Manual handling required."
A swapped-into task can also be rejected (consumes that employee's budget).
Cascading rejections bounded by the per-task ceiling below.
After 3 rejections of the same task, stop the cascade and escalate to admin.
Prevents a single undesirable task from ping-ponging across the workforce.
📨 NOTIFY ADMIN + MANAGER (high · email): "Task Y rejected 3× — handle manually."
Trigger: 3 rejections OR ≥25% of assigned employees in a 4h window
"N rejections in 4h. Cascade paused. Re-optimization preparing."
→ Re-run GA over the affected time window
• In-progress and completed tasks LOCKED (rolling horizon)
📨 NOTIFY ADMIN (high · email): "Re-run done. X reassigned, Y unfulfillable. Approve?"
→ On approval: notify all affected employees of new assignments
Unfulfillable appointments → admin reschedule / cancel decision

svg.text: mass-rejection re-run feeds back into allocation

g.text: required gate before any task transition
Task status: Pending → In Progress · started_at = now()
Feedback row: user_type='employee', rating, keywords, report
Cycle complete · efficiency feeds next optimization
ClientAppointment.status: pending → approved → (assigned) → completed | rejected (terminal, admin-side)
Task.status: Pending → In Progress → Completed | Rejected (preference, triggers cascade)
Rejection budget per employee: 3 / month | per-task ceiling: 3 rejections | window: assignment → 24h before start
Mass-rejection trigger: 3 rejections OR ≥25% of assigned employees in 4h window (whichever is higher)
Re-run scope (rolling horizon): in-progress + completed tasks locked · future-window tasks re-optimizable
app/Services/Notification/NotificationService.php · stage-by-stage notifications to client / admin / employees
The rejection cascade (Try 1 → 2a → 2b → 3), per-task ceiling, and mass-rejection meta-trigger are designed but not yet implemented.
Existing code covers only a thin slice: rejectTask sets Task.status='Rejected' and TaskApprovalObserver recomputes the team's day timetable.
